subject: LU-6210 lnet: Use C99 initializers for struct netstrfns

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1:

I don't think you can delete the definitions for the old LND types until there is no longer any need to interoperate with any old clients or servers, otherwise the NID lookup for the remote node will fail. When did the old LNDs get deleted?

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

The obsolete LND drivers got deleted during the 2.7 development cycle.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Looking at the removed LNDs, I'd think the only one of concern might be the ptllnd, which would be needed for some old Cray client to mount a newer server (the server would need to map the @ptl NID in its routing table to a specific network type).  I don't think there would be any old _servers_ directly attached to a cluster but exported via @ptl NIDs that a new client would connect to.

Since I expect this is a rare case, and I've added Patrick to the review list to see if there are any sites that may need this, I'm OK with the current change, and I'll add a comment to the commit comment to that effect.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch looks good, and we (Cray) think it should be safe to remove the ability for a ptllnd client to connect to a newer server.  The last ptllnd client using machines we released run, I believe, Lustre 1.8, so connecting them to a 2.8 server is questionable at best.

Patch Set 2:

Correct Patrick. In fact Andreas recently submitted a patch (LU-6349) that removed 1.8 support.

Patch Set 5:

James - Could this patch be causing conf-sanity test 43 to fail? The logs to the failure are at: https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/44a4dcda-11b2-11e5-bd2d-5254006e85c2

Patch Set 5:

The array went from 11 entries to 5. So it's possible something is still looking for a old entry, and since it's not there anymore, the search finally hits that last -1 entry. Hence the strlen(NULL) crash. See LU-6501.

Patch Set 5:

If that is the case then we will have to reverse the order of the patches.

Patch Set 5:

The user land code for NID string handling still has the old drivers and the kernel side has removed them. This is exposing the LU-6501 bug.

commit.subject: LU-6210 lnet: Use C99 initializers for struct netstrfns
commit.message:
LU-6210 lnet: Use C99 initializers for struct netstrfns

Update struct netstrfns to use C99 initializers.

Remove old LND types from the netstrfns table, as they are
long obsolete and shouldn't be needed even for interop anymore.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Change-Id: I22cd2d770e0872de343af249d13bf0f56f8a4a7d
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15088
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
